articles.title: Tamil Nadu elections: M K Stalin, EPS, TVK chief Vijay among top leaders cast votes
articles.description: Prominent political leaders, including Chief Minister M K Stalin, C Joseph Vijay, and Edappadi K Palaniswami, cast their votes across Tamil Nadu. Leaders expressed confidence in their respective party's victory, urging citizens to participate in the democratic process. Several leaders also voiced their opinions on the election's significance and potential outcomes.

articles.title: Confident of DMK returning to power, says Udhayanidhi Stalin
articles.description: The DMK, which heads the Secular Progressive Alliance, is seeking to retain power, and is leading a multi-party coalition that includes Congress, Left parties and the VCK.

articles.title: West Bengal elections: EC seeks report on alleged voter intimidation in Murshidabad's Domkal
articles.description: According to the report, several voters in Domkal alleged that they were threatened and intimidated, with some claiming they were warned of assault if they attempted to reach polling stations.

articles.title: Bengal polls phase 1 sees 18.76% early voter turnout; clashes erupt, SIR looms
articles.description: Voting is underway in West Bengal's first phase across 152 constituencies. Early reports indicate peaceful polling with high security. Some minor incidents and EVM issues have been noted. Over 3.60 crore voters are eligible. Key battles feature prominent leaders in various districts. The election focuses on issues like electoral roll revisions and job corruption.

articles.description: Tamil Nadu Polls: PM Modi urges Tamil Nadu & West Bengal voters, especially youth and women, to exercise their democratic right in ongoing assembly elections. Highlights include voting progress in Tamil Nadu and phase-wise polling in West Bengal.

articles.title: Voter Intimidation Charge, Trinamool vs Humayun Kabir As Bengal Votes

articles.title: TN Elections 2026: Sivakumar demands TASMAC abolition after voting
articles.description: Veteran actor Sivakumar cast his vote for the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly polls and urged the next government to abolish TASMAC. Polling is underway with a three-way contest expected between DMK, AIADMK alliances and Vijay's new party TVK.

articles.title: West Bengal Election 2026 Live Updates: North Bengal test for TMC, BJP as 152 seats vote today in first phase amid massive electoral roll purge

Of these 152, as many as 54 are located in eight North Bengal districts while rest are spread across Murshidabad, Nadia, Birbhum and Hooghly.

articles.title: 'Explain in 24 hours': EC notice to Congress leader Mallikarjun Kharge for calling PM Narendra Modi 'terrorist'
articles.description: The Election Commission has formally alerted Congress leader Mallikarjun Kharge with a show-cause notice over his controversial labeling of Prime Minister Narendra Modi as a 'terrorist.' The commission has highlighted that this statement seems to violate established campaign standards and the decorum expected during elections. Kharge is given a 24-hour window to clarify his statements, or he may face penalties.

articles.title: Cash, Liquor, Drugs: EC Seizures Cross Rs 1,000 Crore In Poll-Bound Tamil Nadu And West Bengal
articles.description: The Election Commission of India reported that poll-related seizures in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu have exceeded Rs 1,000 crore during ongoing assembly elections and bypolls. According to the Commission, enforcement agencies have seized inducements worth Rs 1,072.13 crore, including cash, liquor, drugs, precious metals and other freebies allegedly intended to influence voters. Tamil Nadu recorded higher seizures at Rs 599.24 crore, while West Bengal accounted for Rs 472.89 crore. The Commission said extensive monitoring systems have been put in place, including thousands of flying squads and static surveillance teams to ensure compliance with the Model Code of Conduct. It also stressed that citizens and political parties should report violations through the C-Vigil platform to support fair and transparent elections.

articles.title: ‘I am here to testify on budget’: Lutnick dodges Van Hollen’s question on ties with Jeffrey Epstein
articles.description: Howard Lutnick faces explosive Senate questioning over alleged ties to Jeffrey Epstein, as lawmakers accuse him of misleading Americans and dodging key questions. The high-stakes hearing saw tense exchanges, with Lutnick deflecting scrutiny by focusing on the budget instead of addressing Epstein links. The controversy comes amid newly released Epstein files fueling bipartisan outrage and demands for accountability.

articles.title: Nitish reposes trust in Sanjay, re-appoints him as JD(U) no. 2
articles.description: The JD(U) has announced its latest batch of national leaders, with Sanjay Kumar Jha retaining his role as working president. Interestingly, Nishant, the son of party leader Nitish Kumar, was not included this time, even though he joined the party recently.

articles.title: Bihar Police cracks down on cops making reels in uniforms
articles.description: In a bold move to maintain decorum, the Bihar Police has initiated disciplinary action against over 50 officers caught sharing social media reels while in uniform. Leading the charge is Patna, where this violation of the established ban has raised eyebrows.

articles.title: BJP legislators hit out at Congress, RJD for silence over Purnia MP’s remarks
articles.description: In a bold display of solidarity, female members of the BJP and an allied party have fiercely rebuked Purnia MP Pappu Yadav for his alleged disrespectful comments aimed at women in politics, calling for a sincere and unconditional apology.

articles.title: From US To Israel: Global Leaders Mark One Year Of Pahalgam Terror Attack
articles.description: On the first anniversary of the Pahalgam terror attack, US Ambassador to India Sergio Gor and Israeli Ambassador Reuven Azar paid tribute to the 26 victims and expressed solidarity with India. Gor reaffirmed Washington’s support in the fight against terrorism and extended condolences to the affected families. Azar also remembered the victims in a post on social media, stressing that terrorism has no borders and highlighting Israel’s continued support for India. The remembrance marks a moment of shared grief and global unity against terror following the 2025 attack in Jammu and Kashmir.

articles.title: Beijing denies Trump’s ‘gift’ claim over seized ship bound for Iran
articles.description: China denied US claims that a seized ship carried a “gift” for Iran, rejecting allegations of missile-linked shipments as tensions rise over maritime security in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.description: Ahead of elections, over 5,000 Flying Squad Teams (FST) have been pressed into service to respond swiftly to complaints, with a target of resolving issues within 100 minutes.

articles.title: India watches, Pakistan acts: The optics war Delhi is losing
articles.description: Pakistan's diplomatic engagement with Iran is earning praise, contrasting with India's cautious stance. Pakistan's efforts are creating opportunities while India navigates economic and energy challenges. India faces scrutiny over its response to global events and its trade relationship with the US. Diplomatic access and strategic positioning are key themes.

articles.title: Residents pay the price as Afghanistan-Pakistan border remains closed
articles.description: The border between Pakistan and Afghanistan has been completely closed for six months, a direct consequence of the conflict between Kabul and Islamabad. Despite several mediation attempts, negotiations have failed, and the two countries remain at a diplomatic impasse in a war that has already displaced tens of thousands of people and claimed numerous lives.
